title: 8 Herbal Tinctures For Tendinitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can provide relief from tendinitis, a condition characterized by inflammation and pain in the tendons. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.  Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains curcumin, a powerful antioxidant that fights free radicals and reduces inflammation. By consuming a tincture made from these herbs, you can experience relief from tendinitis symptoms.  Achillea millefolium, or yarrow, has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce swelling and pain. Herbal tinctures made from these herbs can be taken orally, allowing the active compounds to be absorbed into the bloodstream and reach the affected area.  Taking herbal tinctures for tendinitis can improve your quality of life by reducing pain and inflammation, allowing you to engage in activities that were previously difficult or impossible. It can also reduce your reliance on pain medication and lower your risk of long-term damage to your tendons. Regular use of these tinctures can also promote overall health and well-being by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.  Herbal tinctures can be made from a combination of herbs, allowing you to tailor your treatment to your specific needs. They can be taken as needed, providing quick relief from tendinitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in the tendons and joints affected by tendinitis. Glycyrrhizin also has a corticosteroid-like effect, which helps suppress the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. This reduction in inflammation and suppression of inflammatory chemicals can help alleviate the symptoms of tendinitis, such as pain and stiffness. The anti-inflammatory and corticosteroid-like effects of glycyrrhizin in Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures make it a potential natural remedy for tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, a glass jar with a lid, and 2 cups of 80% vodka.
Combine the dried root with 2 cups of vodka in the glass jar. Seal the jar with the lid.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day. This helps the plant's active ingredients mix with the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Label the jar with the date and the ingredients used. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, which are powerful anti-inflammatory compounds. The main bioactive constituents, curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain in the body. Curcumin, in particular, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX, which contribute to the development of tendinitis. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, curcuma longa tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of tendinitis, such as pain and stiffness, and promote healing and recovery in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of curcuma longa tinctures make it a potentially effective natural remedy for managing tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Curcuma longa root and 2 cups of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried root and vodka in the jar, making sure the root is completely covered.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the jar and use 15-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve tendinitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures contains sesquiterpene lactones, such as achillol and alantolactone, and flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in tendinitis. The sesquiterpene lactones in Achillea millefolium tinctures can also inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the development of tendinitis. Additionally, the flavonoids in the tincture have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the tendons from oxidative stress and damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the tendons, Achillea millefolium tinctures may provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Combine the herb with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, to help with tendinitis pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tinctures contains sesquiterpene lactones, particularly helenalin and dihydrohelenalin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in tendinitis. Arnica montana also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and chlorogenic acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The tannins and saponins present in Arnica montana help reduce inflammation and pain by blocking the release of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The combination of these bioactive constituents makes Arnica montana tinctures a potential natural treatment for tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Arnica montana flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Arnica flowers to the jar, making sure they are completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator and use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, mixed with water or taken sublingually for tendinitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and pain in tendons, making it easier to move and perform daily tasks. Gingerols and shogaols also have antioxidant effects, which can help protect the damaged tissues from further damage. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Zingiber officinale tinctures, particularly gingerol, inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. By reducing inflammation and protecting damaged tissues, Zingiber officinale tinctures can help alleviate tendinitis symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (ginger) roots. Wash them with water and chop into small pieces.
Combine the chopped ginger with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a glass jar. Make sure the ginger is completely covered by the vodka.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the ginger solids.
Store the ginger tincture in the fridge and use 15-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed to relieve tendinitis pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata tinctures contains boswellic acids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties. These acids, specifically acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA) and 3-acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (3-AKBA), help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major contributor to tendinitis. The boswellic acids also have potent antioxidant properties, which help to prevent further damage to the affected area. Additionally, boswellic acids have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which are involved in the development of tendinitis. By reducing inflammation and preventing further damage, Boswellia serrata tinctures can help alleviate the symptoms of tendinitis.
remedies.image_alt: Boswellia serrata herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Boswellia serrata roots and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried roots and vodka in the jar, making sure the roots are fully covered by the liquid.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve tendinitis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains active constituents like alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, which is common in tendinitis. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to decrease the pain and discomfort associated with tendinitis. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea also have antioxidant effects, which can help to promote healing and repair in the damaged tissues. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may help to alleviate symptoms of tendinitis and support overall joint health.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves. Clean and dry them.
Combine the Echinacea flowers and leaves with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar. Stir well.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, to help with tendinitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures contains flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides, which are powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in tendons, which is a common symptom of tendinitis. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba tinctures also improve blood flow to the affected area, which promotes healing and reduces pain. The bilobalide and ginkgolides have a strong anti-inflammatory effect, which helps reduce pain and stiffness associated with tendinitis. By reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can help alleviate the symptoms of tendinitis and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for tendinitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of vodka (at least 80 proof)
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the vodka.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the leaves release their properties into the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the Ginkgo biloba tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 15-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed for tendinitis relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent tendinitis from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. Regular consumption of these teas might help keep the muscles and tendons in good condition, making them less prone to injury and inflammation.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help with inflammation and pain, consuming them daily for tendinitis is a personal choice. Some teas, like ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that may ease symptoms. However, individual results may vary, and it's essential to consider the quality and preparation of the tea.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with tendinitis, but it's not a quick fix. Some people start feeling better within a few days, while others may take a week or two to notice a difference. It really depends on the individual and the severity of their tendinitis.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for tendinitis is most beneficial when your body is most active. So, have it after a workout or when you're feeling stiff. This can be in the late morning or early afternoon, when your muscles are still warm from activity.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for tendinitis. Some herbs, like ginger and turmeric, can increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood-thinning medications. Ginseng can also make some medications less effective. If you're taking prescription medications, talk to your pharmacist about any herbal teas you're consuming. This will help you avoid potential interactions.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like ginger and peppermint, are usually safe in small amounts. However, others like willow bark, ginkgo biloba, and evening primrose can cause problems or interact with medications. It's also unclear how some herbs affect the baby.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas for tendinitis. Some herbs, like ginger and turmeric, are generally considered safe, but others might not be suitable. For example, willow bark and feverfew can affect blood thinning, which could be a concern for breastfeeding mothers. It's best to check the ingredients carefully.
